response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems, and has worked at meaningful scale (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby). However, the resume shows no evidence of the role's core competencies: experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or growth engineering. There is no mention of statistical methods, experimental analysis, or cross-team influence at the Principal/IC6 scope. The candidate's current trajectory (Senior/Staff individual contributor in full-stack product work) does not align with the systemic, measurement-platform-focused leadership this role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby, processing millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Polyglot engineer with production experience in C, C++, Java, JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, and C# across a wide range of domains
Active knowledge-sharing culture: created workshops, gave conference talks, and ran recurring Refactoring Dojos — evidence of mentorship instinct
concerns: No experimentation, A/B testing, feature flagging, or rollout-risk management experience anywhere in the resume — the foundational requirement of this role
No telemetry infrastructure, metrics pipeline, or experimental analysis background; data aptitude is entirely absent from the work history

Seniority mismatch: current level is Senior/Staff with no evidence of Principal-scope cross-team technical leadership, architecture ownership across multiple workstreams, or stakeholder alignment at org level

key_factors.factor: No telemetry, metrics, or growth engineering background
Multi-domain distributed systems experience at scale
